title: Downloaded Papers Assessment for Indus Script Decipherment

CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.author: Andreas Fuls (2023)
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.relevance: CRITICAL — Definitive sign catalog with positional statistics for all 713 signs. Already using bundled data from this. Full PDF may contain additional tables/appendices not in our extract.
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.action: OCR and extract any sign tables not already in our bundled REAL_CATALOG_SUMMARY data

CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.author: Parpola et al.
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.relevance: CRITICAL — CISI Vol.5 (newest volume). May contain inscriptions and photographs not in our digital corpus. Check for Dholavira and other site data.
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.action: OCR and extract any inscription sequences, especially from sites underrepresented in Holdat

CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.author: Asko Parpola (2010)
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.relevance: CRITICAL — Parpola's definitive Dravidian hypothesis paper. Contains specific sign-phoneme proposals that should be validated against our anchor set.
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.action: Extract Parpola's sign readings table and cross-reference with our 42 anchors

CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.author: S. Kalyanaraman
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.relevance: CRITICAL — Meluhhan trade vocabulary and metalwork semantics. Directly feeds our Mesopotamian cross-reference task.
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.action: Extract all Dravidian-Akkadian word mappings for comparison with our anchor readings

CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.author: Holdat LLC / Kriger & Hunt (2025-2026)
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.relevance: CRITICAL — Semantic scope analysis (taxation, trade licensing, commodity control). Validates our positional grammar discovery.
CRITICAL_USE_IMMEDIATELY.action: Compare their structural analysis with our V5 spectral grid 3-slot grammar finding

HIGH_VALUE.author: Asko Parpola (2005)
HIGH_VALUE.relevance: HIGH — Earlier Parpola study with sign reading proposals
HIGH_VALUE.action: Cross-reference with 2010 paper for evolution of Parpola's readings

HIGH_VALUE.author: Various
HIGH_VALUE.relevance: HIGH — Complete bibliography of Parpola's publications on Indus script
HIGH_VALUE.action: Identify any papers we haven't sourced yet
Extract diachronic data for temporal stratification of sign usage

HIGH_VALUE.relevance: HIGH — Argues for hieroglyphic (logo-syllabic) nature — aligns with our spectral grid finding
HIGH_VALUE.action: Compare their evidence with our spectral clustering results
Cross-reference sign classification with our NWSP positional profiles

HIGH_VALUE.relevance: HIGH — Arguments for true writing system
HIGH_VALUE.action: Use as supporting evidence for our linguistic analysis findings
Compare methodology with our statistical approaches

MODERATE.relevance: MODERATE — Introductory overview, useful for literature review
MODERATE — 12 specific inscriptions with analysis, may include unique sequences
MODERATE — 11 inscriptions, may include sequences not in our corpus
MODERATE — Structural analysis of Harappa inscriptions
MODERATE — Indus script found outside the Indus Valley (Gulf seals!)
MODERATE.action: May contain Gulf-type seal sequences useful for bilingual cross-reference

MODERATE.relevance: MODERATE — Two specific seals with analysis

MODERATE.author: D.T. Potts (2022)
MODERATE.relevance: MODERATE — Iranian plateau graffiti potentially related to Indus contact
MODERATE — Response to Sproat's non-script argument

SUPPLEMENTARY.relevance: SUPPLEMENTARY — Vedic knowledge system interpretation (alternative theory)

VEDIC_STUDIES_SUBFOLDER.relevance: Petteri Koskikallio (CISI co-editor) papers on Vedic studies — may contain relevant archaeological context
VEDIC_STUDIES_SUBFOLDER.action: Low priority for direct decipherment, but useful for cultural context

recommended_immediate_actions: OCR the Fuls 2023 Catalog PDF to extract any sign tables not in bundled data
Extract Parpola 2010 sign readings and compare with our 42 anchors
Extract Meluhha metalwork vocabulary for Mesopotamian cross-reference
Check 'Indus Script in Warka/Uruk/Susa' paper for Gulf seal sequences
Compare Holdat/Kriger semantic scope analysis with our positional grammar
